candidate_summary: Dual-band gradient attenuation inspired by Punjabi bhangra dhol drum: g_t decomposed into low-frequency g_L (long-EMA) + high-frequency g_H (short-EMA); independent attenuators α_L, α_H per phrase; at high-curvature steps α_L→0 (bass-mute); Chaal 4-step cyclical bass-treble balance schedule.

results.name: AdEMAMix Optimizer OpenReview
results.rationale: AdEMAMix decomposes gradient via two EMAs (slow+fast) feeding update; functionally equivalent to g_L+g_H decomposition with separate weighting.

results.name: AdEMAMix Apple ML
results.rationale: Dual-EMA Adam variant; same dual-band gradient decomposition mechanism as bass/treble split.

results.name: HELENE Hessian Layer-wise Annealing (2411.10696)
results.rationale: Curvature-triggered clipping + EMA annealing partially overlaps high-curvature attenuation but single-band, no dual decomposition.

results.name: AdamW EMA Time-Varying LR (ICLR 2025)
results.rationale: Time-varying LR with EMA; no frequency decomposition or curvature gating.

results.name: Gradient Descent Algorithm Survey (2511.20725)
results.rationale: General survey, no specific dual-band attenuation mechanism.

results.name: Hessian Layer-wise Clipping (EMNLP 2025)
results.rationale: Curvature-aware clipping with annealing; lacks dual-band independent attenuators.

results.name: Cyclical Loss-Based Pretraining (KBS 2025)
results.rationale: Cyclical schedule overlaps Chaal cyclicality but on loss thresholds, not dual-band bass-mute.

results.name: Adam Model EMA Nonconvex (2405.18199)
results.rationale: Adam+model EMA for variance reduction; single EMA track, not dual-band decomposition.

decision_rationale: AdEMAMix (results 1,2) implements dual-EMA gradient decomposition (slow baseline + fast deviation) with independent weighting, which is functionally equivalent (func=0.72) to the proposed g_L/g_H dual-band attenuation core. The bhangra-dhol framing (Chaal cyclicality, curvature-gated bass-mute) adds scheduling on top but the fundamental dual-band attenuator mechanism is anticipated. Verdict: FAIL.
